AAPL.ai_peer_assessment: AAPL trades at a 29.4% premium to peer median P/E of 28.4x—driven by superior profitability (146.7% ROE vs median 33.2%) but unjustifiable given growth trajectory. The 358% premium on P/B (42.3x vs 9.2x median) is extreme and reflects capital allocation (buybacks inflating ROE) rather than intrinsic advantage. On EV/EBITDA, AAPL's 28.4x is 34.8% above peer median 21.0x, a significant premium for a company with slowing revenue growth. P/S multiple of 9.97x is -7.3% discount to peers, suggesting h

MSFT.ai_peer_assessment: MSFT trades at a 25.7% discount to the peer median P/E of 36.8x (occupied by AAPL at 36.8x). On P/B, MSFT's 8.25x is a stunning 80.5% discount to median 42.31x, reflecting superior capital efficiency and lower asset intensity. On P/S, MSFT is essentially flat to peers at 10.75x vs median 10.77x, indicating software pricing parity. The EV/EBITDA of 17.15x is 39.5% below the 28.36x median, the most attractive valuation lever, suggesting the market is willing to pay less per dollar of cash earnings

JPM.ai_peer_assessment: JPM trades at 14.04x PE vs peer median of 14.02x—essentially AT MARKET. However, this masks JPM's structural superiority: 16.3% ROE vs peer 11.1% median, 26.2% op margin vs 20.4% median. Comparables: BAC (11.8x PE, 10.5% ROE), Citigroup (14.0x PE, 7.5% ROE), CIBC (13.7x PE, 15.2% ROE). JPM should trade at 15.5-16.0x PE premium to BAC (justified by 560bp ROE/margin advantage), implying fair value $320-330. The 47.5% PB premium (vs 1.54x median) is appropriate given JPM's superior returns profile.
